title: KPI Framework — Measuring AI Transformation Success
subtitle: Baselined before deployment · Measured at 30/60/90/180 days · Results feed future RFx criteria

capabilities.name: Feature Requirement
capabilities.kpi: Days from idea to approved Gate 1 demand

capabilities.name: Business Intent Translator
capabilities.kpi: Days from discussion to Gate 2-ready structured spec

capabilities.name: Tech-Spec Sparring
capabilities.kpi: Days from scope intake to architecture baseline approved

capabilities.name: Builder / Coding
capabilities.kpi: Days from spec to merged, deployable code; PR turnaround hrs

capabilities.name: Test & Quality Assurance
capabilities.kpi: Manual test hours per sprint; defect escape rate to production

portfolio.name: Cost per Feature Point
portfolio.definition: Total IT&AI project spend / feature points delivered per quarter

portfolio.name: Project Overrun Rate
portfolio.definition: % of projects exceeding budget or schedule baseline
portfolio.baseline: Should decline as estimation accuracy improves (cap 2)

portfolio.name: SIT/UAT Rework Rate
portfolio.definition: % of build output requiring rework in test phases
portfolio.baseline: Should decline as spec quality improves (caps 3, 4)

portfolio.name: Vendor AI Adoption Score
portfolio.definition: % of vendor dev hours using AI tools (Copilot telemetry + sprint logs)
portfolio.baseline: Tracked from July 2026; target >80% by Q4 2026

portfolio.name: Knowledge Base Health Score
portfolio.definition: % of indexed documents with last-updated date <90 days

portfolio.name: AI Output Override Rate
portfolio.definition: % of AI suggestions accepted vs. overridden by humans
portfolio.baseline: Healthy range indicates tool calibration; extreme lows = under-review risk

rfxIntegration.headline: Feeding Results into Future RFx
rfxIntegration.text: From Q3 2026, all new RFx documents must include: (1) vendor's demonstrated AI tool adoption rate from prior UPM projects; (2) baseline KPI improvement vs. non-AI baseline; (3) proposed AI tooling plan and efficiency gain commitment. AI capability weighted at 20% of technical scoring.
